2604.01216.title: LAtent Phase Inference from Short time sequences using SHallow REcurrent Decoders (LAPIS-SHRED)
2604.01216.paper_summary: Reconstructing full spatio-temporal dynamics from sparse observations in both space and time remains a central challenge in complex systems, as measurements can be spatially incomplete and can be also limited to narrow temporal windows. Yet approximating the complete spatio-temporal trajectory is essential for mechanistic insight and understanding, model calibration, and operational decision-making. We introduce LAPIS-SHRED (LAtent Phase Inference from Short time sequence using SHallow REcurrent Decoders), a modular architecture that reconstructs and/or forecasts complete spatiotemporal dynamics from sparse sensor observations confined to short temporal windows. LAPIS-SHRED operates through a three-stage pipeline: (i) a SHRED model is pre-trained entirely on simulation data to map sensor time-histories into a structured latent space, (ii) a temporal sequence model, trained on simulation-derived latent trajectories, learns to propagate latent states forward or backward in time to span unobserved temporal regions from short observational time windows, and (iii) at deployment, only a short observation window of hyper-sparse sensor measurements from the true system is provided, from which the frozen SHRED model and the temporal model jointly reconstruct or forecast the complete spatiotemporal trajectory. The framework supports bidirectional inference, inherits data assimilation and multiscale reconstruction capabilities from its modular structure, and accommodates extreme observational constraints including single-frame terminal inputs. We evaluate LAPIS-SHRED on six experiments spanning complex spatio-temporal physics: turbulent flows, multiscale propulsion physics, volatile combustion transients, and satellite-derived environmental fields, highlighting a lightweight, modular architecture suited for operational settings where observation is constrained by physical or logistical limitations.
2604.01216.paper_summary_zh: 重建來自空間和時間的稀疏觀測的完整空間-時間動態仍然是複雜系統中的一個核心挑戰，因為測量可能在空間上不完整，並且也可能僅限於狹窄的時間窗口。<lb/>然而，近似完整的空間-時間軌跡對於機制洞察和理解、模型校準以及操作決策至關重要。<lb/>我們介紹了LAPIS-SHRED（LAtent Phase Inference from Short time sequence using SHallow REcurrent Decoders），這是一種模組化架構，能夠從限於短時間窗口的稀疏傳感器觀測中重建和/或預測完整的空間-時間動態。<lb/>LAPIS-SHRED通過三個階段的流程運作：（i）SHRED模型完全在模擬數據上進行預訓練，以將傳感器時間歷史映射到結構化的潛在空間，（ii）一個時間序列模型，基於模擬衍生的潛在軌跡進行訓練，學習在時間上向前或向後推進潛在狀態，以涵蓋來自短觀測時間窗口的未觀察時間區域，以及（iii）在部署時，只提供來自真實系統的超稀疏傳感器測量的短觀測窗口，從中凍結的SHRED模型和時間模型共同重建或預測完整的空間-時間軌跡。<lb/>該框架支持雙向推斷，繼承了其模組化結構的數據同化和多尺度重建能力，並能夠適應極端觀測限制，包括單幀終端輸入。<lb/>我們在六個實驗中評估了LAPIS-SHRED，這些實驗涵蓋了複雜的空間-時間物理：湍流、跨尺度推進物理、波動燃燒瞬態和衛星衍生的環境場，突顯了適合於觀測受物理或後勤限制的操作環境的輕量級模組化架構。
2604.01216.author: Yuxuan Bao et.al.
2604.01216.authors: Yuxuan Bao, Xingyue Zhang, J. Nathan Kutz
